scope: DELTA-ONLY review of commit 0ce2ec0 (behavioral-spec.md: DOC-PARITY-004..007, DOC-PARITY-005a, DOC-README-003a; verification-architecture.md: PROP-023..025, revised Purity boundary §1 and Budget guard §3 for two-wallet accounting). Sections that already passed iteration-5 are NOT re-litigated.

advisory.finding: The MCP-side spend cap is stated three times as '≈$0.002 × 4 = ≈$0.008 total' for the four paid DUAL-LIVE-RUN commands (defi/markets/rpc/phone). I ground-truthed each individual cost directly against VERIFICATION.md's own ledger rows (grep -n, not trusting the spec's citation): row 6 defi=$0.001, row 8 markets=$0.001, row 9 rpc=$0.002, row 18 phone=$0.001 -- these are the SAME four figures behavioral-spec.md's DOC-PARITY-005a itself states inline. The real sum is $0.001+$0.001+$0.002+$0.001 = $0.005, not $0.008 -- the '$0.002 x 4' formula silently applies the highest individual price (rpc's $0.002) to all four instead of summing the actual per-command costs. This is a genuine arithmetic inconsistency between the cap figure and the per-command figures stated two lines away in the same documents. It is, however, in the SAFE direction for money-safety purposes (it overstates the funding needed/spend cap rather than understating available balance or the real cost -- unlike iteration-4's since-fixed FIND-004-001, which was an unsound OPTIMISTIC estimate that risked authorizing a spend beyond real balance). Same category and severity as iteration-5's carried-over non-blocking advisories (ADV-005-TOPUP-CONFIRM-BUDGET-ARITHMETIC-STILL-LOOSE) -- a documentation-precision nit, not a money-safety or contradiction risk. Non-blocking.

PROP-023 (the new MCP-vs-CLI dual-invocation parity check) is placed under the pre-existing section heading '### Tier 2 -- live execution of README examples (real API, free-path preferred)'. PROP-023 does not execute a README example -- it invokes a connected mcp__blockrun__blockrun_<name> tool AND the CLI command side-by-side for parity comparison, an operation the heading text does not describe. The heading was not updated when PROP-023 was inserted under it. This is a categorization-label clarity nit (a future reader skimming the heading would not expect PROP-023's content there); it does not create a contradiction with PROP-023's own body text, which is unambiguous about what it does. Non-blocking.

Of the 9 DUAL-LIVE-RUN commands, 5 (wallet/chat/models/dex/price) have concrete, already-executed example invocations transcribed verbatim in both spec files. The remaining 4 paid ones (defi/markets/rpc/phone) have no pre-specified exact invocation (path/params) for the dual-run -- the spec explicitly defers exact input selection to Phase 3/4 ('SHALL perform the same dual-invocation-and-compare procedure' without naming the specific --path/--params each side will use). For a valid parity comparison the CLI and MCP call must use IDENTICAL semantic input, so Phase 3/4's executor will need to choose matching parameters itself. This is analogous to already-accepted advisories in this feature's history (e.g. ADV-005-TOPUP-SEND-TOOL-UNNAMED) where a live-execution procedural detail is left to the executing agent's judgment rather than hardcoded in the spec; acceptable for a Tier-2 (live, agent-judgment) proof obligation, not a Tier-1 mechanical one. Non-blocking.

verifiedIndependently: 9/9 DUAL-LIVE-RUN/SCHEMA-ONLY partition is EXHAUSTIVE and NON-OVERLAPPING: cross-checked both lists (wallet,chat,models,dex,price,defi,markets,rpc,phone / image,video,music,realface,modal,speech,search,exa,surf) against the CLI's actual 18 registered commands in src/index.ts (grep -F '.command(' src/index.ts, 18 matches, real command names extracted directly from source, not from the spec's own claim) -- exact 1:1 match, no command missing or double-counted.

Traceability totals recounted mechanically (not trusted from the spec's own '50/25' claim): grep -oE for all DOC-*/REQ-NG-* IDs in behavioral-spec.md yields 55 unique IDs = 50 DOC-* + 5 REQ-NG-* (DOC-* only, matching the spec's '50 DOC-* requirements' claim exactly); grep -oE for all **PROP-N** definitions in verification-architecture.md yields exactly 25 unique PROP IDs, matching the claimed '25 PROPs' exactly. Traceability-summary table's own per-group arithmetic (14+3+6+2+7+8+1+3+6=50) also recomputed and confirmed correct.

The MCP models-count discrepancy (44 vs 55 for identical category:chat filter) is recorded HONESTLY and IDENTICALLY in both behavioral-spec.md (DOC-PARITY-005) and verification-architecture.md (PROP-023) -- not hidden, not silently 'corrected' in either doc, explicitly deferred to the CLI feature owner without touching src/ (respects REQ-NG-001) and framed as a documentation finding not a docs-feature bug (respects DOC-CONSTRAINT-002's no-lies rule). No drift between the two mirrored descriptions.

Two-wallet budget separation (CLI sandbox wallet A vs MCP-connected wallet B) is explicit and consistent in §1's Purity boundary table and §3's Budget guard: both state the two spends are 'never netted against each other', PROP-023's spend is 'entirely independent of PROP-020's sandbox budget', and both wallet addresses/balances (Base 0x99b3fE1Ef8Fd94AfA5FF3448B3d7f05372cFa94e=$0, Solana 8FpqdcCHqjqkVXR58eVJa53neXbJf9emXhvHhgeUPCV9=$0, active chain solana) are stated identically in DOC-PARITY-005a and PROP-023/§3. No double-spend, no netting error, no contradiction with REQ-220/REQ-221 money-safety properties.

PROP-023's tool names (mcp__blockrun__blockrun_<name> for all 9 DUAL-LIVE-RUN commands) correspond 1:1 to this session's actually-connected MCP tool set (cross-checked against the 18 mcp__blockrun__blockrun_* tools listed in this session's own deferred-tools reminder) -- no invented tool name.
PARITY.md correctly does NOT yet exist on disk (ls confirms) -- consistent with the feature still being in spec phase (1b/1c), since PARITY.md is a Phase-2/3+ artifact per DOC-PARITY-001.

DOC-README-003a is confirmed to be positioning-only (how the README frames its primary reader) and explicitly self-labels as not adding a new technical requirement -- no conflict with REQ-NG-002/003/004 or any existing DOC-README-* requirement.

DOC-PARITY-007's removal of codex-review as a required gate is explicitly scoped to 'this feature' only, cites Dais's direct instruction (consistent with the project's own stated precedence order where a Dais directive outranks project-level process rules), and retains historical codex-review findings verbatim as an audit trail rather than erasing them -- no contradiction with the rest of the project's codex-review policy, which is unaffected outside this one feature.

newBlockingScan.scope: Full delta of commit 0ce2ec0: behavioral-spec.md DOC-PARITY-001..007/-005a and DOC-README-003a (§6, lines ~220-330 and §1 line ~73-81); verification-architecture.md PROP-023/024/025, §1 Purity boundary, §3 Budget guard (lines ~1-42, 125-137, 158-192, 318-378). Plus consistency check against untouched pre-existing REQs/PROPs (budget guard, no-double-spend, src-read-only, no-lies rules) and a mechanical recount of both ID sets against real source (src/index.ts) and real cost data (VERIFICATION.md).

note: Spec DELTA review, iteration 6, fresh adversary instance with zero Builder context, reviewing ONLY the new material added per Dais's direct instruction on top of the already-PASSed iteration-5 spec. All independent verification was performed by reading real source/data files directly (src/index.ts, VERIFICATION.md, this session's own connected MCP tool list) rather than trusting the spec's self-citations. Three non-blocking advisories recorded (arithmetic-cap looseness in the safe/conservative direction, a stale Tier-2 section heading that doesn't describe PROP-023's actual content, and unspecified exact inputs for 4 of the 9 dual-live-run commands deferred to Phase 3/4 agent judgment) -- none rise to blocking under this feature's own established bar (compare: iteration-5 carried two similarly-shaped advisories forward as non-blocking without re-litigating them). Verdict: PASS on both dimensions. This delta does not reopen or contradict anything already passed in iteration-5; the feature remains ready to proceed to Phase 2a (TDD/RED) contingent on any prior remaining gates.
